_comment: AI Daily Report - Source Configuration. Two dimensions: quality (model-scored 1-10) and crawl_difficulty (easy/medium/hard).

sources.name: Hacker News Top Stories
sources.description: HN top stories via Firebase API. Returns story IDs, fetch each item for title/url/score/comments.
sources.notes: Firebase API is public, no auth needed. Rate limit friendly. Top 500 story IDs returned, filter by score >= 50 for quality.

sources.name: Hacker News Best Stories
sources.description: HN best stories (algorithmically ranked). Higher signal-to-noise than top stories.
sources.notes: Best stories have higher average quality. Use as primary source, supplement with top stories for recency.

sources.name: HuggingFace Daily Papers
sources.description: HuggingFace curated daily papers with upvotes. API returns JSON with paper metadata and community votes.
sources.notes: API may return HTML if Accept header is wrong. Use WebFetch with prompt to extract structured data. Papers are already community-curated by upvotes.

easy.description: Direct API call via curl. Returns clean JSON. No auth, no JS rendering needed.

medium.description: Needs HTTP fetch with content extraction. May return HTML that needs parsing, or API with specific headers.
medium.example: Use WebFetch tool with a prompt to extract structured data from the page.

hard.description: Requires full browser automation. JS-rendered content, login walls, infinite scroll, or anti-bot measures.
hard.example: Navigate to page, wait for JS render, extract visible text, screenshot if needed.

tier_1.label: Primary sources
tier_1.description: Community-curated, high signal-to-noise. Always include in daily report.

tier_2.label: Secondary sources
tier_2.description: Good content but may need heavier filtering. Include if time permits or for specific topics.

tier_3.label: Supplementary sources
tier_3.description: Niche or noisy. Only use for gap-filling on specific topics.
